release-date.comment: Initially announced January 2018; early-adopter pre-orders opened end of April 2018, and general availability was reported in June 2018.

status.comment: Pine64 declared LTS (long-term supply) commitment until at least 2023. Both Pine64 store variants (2GB, 4GB) showed Out of Stock as of 2026-04-26; major resellers (Ameridroid) also out of stock. Still listed as a current product on pine64.org/devices, no formal discontinuation announced.

architecture-family.comment: Rockchip RK3399 is an ARMv8-A 64-bit hexa-core SoC.

open-source-hardware.comment: Pine64 publishes schematics PDFs (v2.0, v2.1) and DXF board outlines, but full PCB layout/design files are not released under an OSHW-compatible license. Per RESEARCH.md, schematics-only does not qualify.

country-of-origin.comment: Pine64 is a Pine Microsystems Inc. project; the Pine Store / community is operated out of Hong Kong (with manufacturing in mainland China).

max-clock-speed-ghz.comment: A72 cluster up to 2.0 GHz; A53 cluster up to 1.5 GHz. Max stock clock taken from A72.

process-node.comment: RK3399 is fabricated on a 28 nm HKMG process.

opengl-es-version.comment: Hardware supports OpenGL ES 1.1/1.2/2.0/3.1/3.2 per Mali-T860 vendor specs; mainline Panfrost driver may not expose all features.

vulkan-support.comment: Mali-T860 supports Vulkan 1.0 per ARM datasheet; in practice usable via mainline Panfrost or vendor blobs.

npu.comment: RK3399 (used here) has no dedicated NPU. The RK3399Pro variant has an NPU but is not used on the RockPro64.

hw-video-decode.comment: Hardware decode for H.264, H.265 (HEVC), and VP9 up to 4K@60. No AV1 decode (RK3399 predates AV1 hardware support).

hw-video-encode.comment: RK3399 video encoder supports H.264 up to 1080p@30. No H.265, VP9, or AV1 hardware encode.

emmc.comment: eMMC module connector onboard; modules sold separately up to 128 GB.

m2-nvme-slot.comment: No native M.2 slot on the PCB. NVMe SSDs are used via the open-ended PCIe 2.1 x4 slot with a PCIe-to-M.2 adapter card.

sata.comment: No native SATA. SATA only via add-in PCIe card (e.g., the Pine64 PCIe-SATA card with ASMedia ASM1061).

ethernet.comment: Single Gigabit Ethernet via Realtek RTL8211F PHY.

wifi.comment: No onboard Wi-Fi. Pine64 sells optional 802.11ac modules (older 2×2 MIMO with BT 4.x, newer 1×1 with BT 5.0) that connect via the dedicated module header.

bluetooth.comment: No onboard Bluetooth. Provided only via the optional Wi-Fi/BT add-on module (BT 4.x or 5.0 depending on module revision).

usb-c-ports-data.comment: 1× USB 3.0 Type-C with DisplayPort 1.2 alt mode (data + DP); not used for power input.

poe-support.comment: No PoE on the Ethernet port and no PoE HAT pinout in Pine64's accessory catalog for this board.

hdmi-outputs.comment: 1× HDMI 2.0 output up to 4K@60. Wiki notes a single HDMI port (RK3399 internally has two VOPs but only one HDMI is broken out).

max-display-resolution.comment: HDMI 2.0 4096×2160 @ 60 Hz; secondary VOP supports up to 2560×1600 via eDP/MIPI-DSI.

displayport.comment: DisplayPort 1.2 available via the USB-C port (DP-Alt mode); no separate native DP connector.

audio-jack.comment: 3.5 mm stereo jack plus separate 3-pin S/PDIF header.

rpi-compatible-header.comment: Form factor matches Pi 2×20 layout, but pin assignments differ from Raspberry Pi GPIO; many HATs that depend on RPi-specific pin mapping will not work without remapping.

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: Mechanically incompatible with RPi HAT spec (different board outline ~133×80 mm vs Pi 85×56 mm; mounting holes are not the Pi pattern). No HAT EEPROM auto-config.

pcie-lanes-exposed.comment: Full open-ended physical x4 slot wired to the RK3399's PCIe 2.1 controller; effective speed limited to PCIe Gen1 in many configurations per vendor docs.

i2c-spi-uart.comment: I²C, SPI, UART, and I²S all available on the Pi-2 header.

power-input.comment: Pine64 sells a 12V/5A version recommended for use with PCIe HDDs/SSDs.

idle-power-w.comment: Forum-reported idle ~3.1 W with eMMC, HDMI display attached, no PCIe peripherals (user 'lucho'); other reporters saw ~2.3-4.2 W depending on attached storage. Snapshot reading.

load-power-w.comment: Forum-reported ~8.5 W under sysbench CPU load (user 'Bullet64'); peaks of 11 W observed under heavier 6-thread loads. No vendor-published number.

cooling-required.comment: Pine64 sells and recommends a passive aluminium heatsink (with optional fan); board ships without cooling and will throttle quickly under sustained A72 load without one.

rtc-battery-header.comment: Dedicated RTC battery backup header; CR-2032 holder accessory sold separately by Pine64.

form-factor-class.comment: 133×80 mm is well outside RPi-standard (~85×56 mm) and does not match Pico-ITX (100×72) or Nano-ITX. Treated as a custom Pine64 outline.

mounting-hole-pattern.comment: Mounting holes are not Pi-pattern. Heatsink mounting holes around the RK3399 are 59 mm apart (Pine64-specific).

weight-g.comment: 0.116 kg per Pine Store product spec; bare board without heatsink or accessories.

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: Mature mainline support. The rk3399-rockpro64.dts has been in the upstream Linux kernel for years; Armbian ships images on current 6.18.x kernel as of April 2026 with Ethernet, USB, HDMI, GPIO, PCIe all working. GPU acceleration relies on Panfrost (mainline).

official-os-image.comment: Pine64 wiki Software Releases page links community-maintained Debian/Armbian images and vendor Android builds.

distros-with-images.comment: Debian (Bullseye/Bookworm), Armbian (Ubuntu 24.04 + Debian 13 builds on current kernel), Manjaro ARM, DietPi, postmarketOS (Alpine-based) all publish board-specific images. Wiki does not list a board-specific Ubuntu image directly, and Fedora/openSUSE have no dedicated RockPro64 image. Also covered: LibreELEC, Batocera, OpenWrt, Slackware, NixOS, Void Linux, NetBSD, FreeBSD, OpenBSD.

bootloader.comment: U-Boot (mainline support; rockpro64-rk3399_defconfig in upstream U-Boot). Boots from SPI flash, eMMC, microSD, or USB.

long-term-support.comment: Pine64 originally committed to LTS supply through 2023+. Board has been continuously available for 7+ years (2018-2026) and the SoC has long-term mainline kernel support; vendor-stocked supply is now thin (out of stock at Pine Store), but software longevity is among the best on the SBC market for RK3399.

android-support.comment: Vendor-listed Android 7.1.2, 8.1.0, and 9.0.0 images for both eMMC and microSD. No newer Android builds maintained.

msrp-base-usd.comment: 2GB community price as listed at pine64.com on 2026-04-26 (out of stock).

msrp-top-usd.comment: 4GB community price as listed at pine64.com on 2026-04-26 (out of stock). Resellers like Ameridroid list ~$99.95.

available-regions.comment: Pine64 ships from a global store and EU store; Ameridroid covers North America; Botland covers Europe; Indian distributor Fab.to.Lab also stocks. No widely-distributed India-direct or LATAM-specific channels.

typical-lead-time.comment: Snapshot 2026-04-26: Pine Store (both 2GB and 4GB) and Ameridroid all show Out of Stock. Worth flagging — board may be drifting toward EOL despite Pine64's earlier LTS pledge.

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive vendor wiki, full schematic PDFs, layout files, datasheet links for the SoC and peripherals; getting-started guides exist. Slightly behind RPi-tier polish, but excellent by SBC standards.

community-size.comment: Active Pine64 forum subforum and dedicated Armbian RockPro64 subforum; per RESEARCH.md threshold, Pine64 fits 'medium' (established second-tier vendor).

active-forum-discord.comment: Vendor-run forum.pine64.org and official IRC/Matrix/Discord-equivalent chat channels remain active in 2026.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: Strong vendor-published accessory line: NAS-style metal case, playbox/desktop case, passive heatsink + fan, PCIe-to-SATA card, NVMe adapter, Wi-Fi/BT modules, RTC battery holder, eMMC modules, USB-PD trigger, IR receiver. Light third-party HAT ecosystem because pinout is not Pi-compatible.
